prompt: This method reads a file but leaks the file handle if readLine() throws. What is the best fix?
choices: Call br.flush() before br.close() to ensure all data is written
Wrap the reader in try-with-resources so it closes automatically even if readLine() throws
Declare the method to catch IOException internally instead of declaring throws
Use FileReader directly instead of wrapping it in BufferedReader
explanation: If readLine() throws, execution jumps past br.close() and the file handle is never released — a resource leak that can cause 'too many open files' errors. try-with-resources guarantees close() is called whether the block completes normally or via exception. Fix: try (BufferedReader br = new BufferedReader(new FileReader(path))) { return br.readLine(); }
